mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts (HOUST) below its trailing 12-month peak signals contracting residential investment and household demand, which historically precedes broader consumption and equity earnings weakness, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 20% below its trailing 12-month peak level
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1331k in Aug2007 is sharply below 2005-06 housing boom peaks above 2000k
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns following HOUST declines exceeding the trigger threshold are positive in more than 55% of historical instances, or Rank IC stays below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained downward trend in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) over the prior 3 months lowers the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows and eases credit conditions, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 4.94 in Sep2007 reflects a cut from prior 5.25 target
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns during FEDFUNDS downtrend periods are not significantly higher than the unconditional baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of decelerating nonfarm payroll growth (PAYEMS) and a rising unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals an inflecting labor market that forward-looking equity markets price as a leading recession indicator, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days, an effect not captured by either series alone.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month annualized growth falls below 1% while UNRATE rises simultaneously
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 138265k and UNRATE at 4.7% as of Sep2007, near cycle peak employment
failure_condition: Joint PAYEMS deceleration and UNRATE rise instances produce positive or flat 20-day forward returns in more than 55% of cases, or the interaction term adds no incremental Rank IC over single-series baselines.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Reserve cuts FEDFUNDS while housing starts (HOUST) continue to decline, markets interpret the easing as reactive rather than preemptive, signaling deeper underlying demand weakness; this joint pattern produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days despite the accommodative policy stance.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS declines over 3 months while HOUST simultaneously falls more than 10% year-over-year
evidence_refs: fact:Fed cut to 4.94 in Sep2007 concurrent with HOUST at 1331k, well below prior year levels
regime_hint: late-cycle housing downturn with monetary easing (2007-08 style credit unwind)
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of periods matching the joint trigger, or the interaction effect fails to outperform either single-series FEDFUNDS or HOUST models on Rank IC.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in CPI (CPIAUCSL) raises inflation expectations and reduces the probability of near-term Fed rate cuts, tightening the implied real discount rate on equities and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following CPI acceleration months are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained expansion in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects healthy supply-side output and corporate earnings momentum, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days through the standard trend-following link between real output growth and equity valuations.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 114.4254 as of Aug2007, near cycle highs
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns during INDPRO uptrends are not statistically different from unconditional baseline (p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month surge in retail sales (RSAFS) above a defined threshold signals resilient consumer spending that supports near-term corporate revenue expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 380230 million dollars as of Sep2007
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns after RSAFS surges above threshold are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion where FEDFUNDS remains elevated above 4.5% while UNRATE begins to tick up from cycle lows, the combination signals restrictive monetary policy persisting into a weakening labor market, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as markets price rising recession risk.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 4.5% while UNRATE rises at least 0.2pp from its trailing 6-month low
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 4.94 and UNRATE at 4.7% as of Sep2007, up modestly from 2007 lows near 4.4-4.5%
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with restrictive monetary policy
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns during joint trigger periods are positive in more than 55% of instances, or the interaction model fails to beat single-series FEDFUNDS or UNRATE baselines on Rank IC.
